news.body: President Valen opened the spring session of Parliament with a call for “steadiness in a loud decade”. The opposition benches, newly confident after autumn polling, promised to be anything but steady. The budget, the Kordi question and the strait dominate the order paper.
news.author: Jana Halden
news.headline: Parliament opens the 1962 session

news.body: At a crowded showroom on Assembly Street, LEIKA chief Kira Moss switched on the Republic’s first mass-market television. “Every Arcasian living room,” she promised, “within the decade.” The set retails at K148. The Herald’s reviewer notes the picture is excellent and the cabinet walnut.
news.author: Jana Halden
news.headline: LEIKA unveils the T-1 television set

cities.name: Lachevan
cities.description: Federal capital of the Republic of Arcasia. Seat of Parliament, the Presidency and the great ministries.

cities.name: Port Kradon
cities.description: Western gateway of the Republic. Shipyards, docks and the Grazihall works.

cities.name: Razno
cities.description: Inland industrial seat of the east. Ageing refineries, the SATROM plant and rows of idle workshops — once the busiest manufacturing town in Arcasia, now working well below capacity.

cities.name: Surat
cities.description: Administrative seat of the semi-autonomous Kordi region.

cities.name: Mezdov
cities.description: Highland mining town and provincial seat.

cities.name: Cape Valgos
cities.description: Harbour town on the Strait of Valgos.

cities.name: Kradesh
cities.description: Market town at the Lachevan road intersection, where the western approach from Kradon meets the federal road into the capital. A busy waystation of hauliers, coaching inns and freight sheds.

cities.name: Grazile
cities.description: Junction town of southern Grazi, where the western road meets the highland passes.

cities.name: Kradeno
cities.description: Crossroads town where the Lachevan, Grazi and Korota roads meet.

events.name: Jobs → Employment
events.description: STEP 1 of the causal chain. Provincial employment is recomputed from labour demand (Σ employees of every property in the province) against the working-age labour force. Blend keeps it gradual; k calibrates demand to the listed workforce sample. Tune k / workingShare / blend here.

events.name: Employment → Happiness
events.description: STEP 2. Provincial GDP follows employment; happiness drifts toward an employment-anchored target (35 + employment·0.35), nudged by approval. Unemployment is the strongest depressor of mood — it outweighs approval roughly 2:1.

events.name: Household Confidence
events.description: Each population group’s economic confidence drifts toward provincial employment conditions.

events.name: Economy → Opinion
events.description: STEP 3. Government support in every demographic group moves with local happiness, household economic confidence, and national GDP growth (g(gdpGrowth)), plus a little noise. Approval of the government is the slower-moving cousin of happiness.

events.name: Trust → Companies
events.description: STEP 4. Each company’s citizen trust drifts toward the average happiness of the provinces where it holds property. Trust feeds share prices (the weekly market session) and can be used as a small revenue multiplier.

events.name: Monthly Census & Migration
events.description: Populations grow slowly; the census bureau updates provincial totals.

events.name: Kordi Unrest Watch

effects.body: Reports from Surat describe growing frustration in the southern region as approval of the federal government falls. Kordish Freedom Front organisers were seen outside the Grand Bazaar.
effects.headline: Unrest simmers in Kordi

events.description: If approval in Kordi collapses below 30, crime rises and support bleeds. A threshold event — dormant until conditions are met.

events.name: General Election
events.description: Dissolve Parliament and go to the country. Results are computed from the simulated population of every province.

events.name: State Infrastructure Programme

effects.body: The Ministry of Development will spend K25 million on roads, water and power across all five provinces. Grazihall shares rose on the announcement.
effects.headline: Government launches nationwide works programme

events.description: A K25M national works programme. Fire manually when the government announces it.

events.name: Strait of Valgos Incident

effects.body: Valkslandic patrol vessels shadowed an Arcasian convoy off Cape Valgos for six hours before withdrawing. The Admiralty called the incident “regrettable”. Shipping insurers called it expensive.
effects.headline: Naval standoff in the Strait of Valgos

events.description: A naval standoff in the strait. Trade suffers; the government answers hard questions. Fire manually for drama.

markers.title: Casa City
markers.description: Capital of Del'Casia. Once a part of our Arcasia, now an industrial park of concrete.

markers.title: Del'Casian Trade Hub
markers.description: Our trade hub with the Del'Casians. Not much trade these days.

markers.title: Malport
markers.description: Capital of Mazon, major trading hub.

markers.title: Madroninburg
markers.description: Capital of Madrosia, major trading hub.

entities.name: Government of Arcasia
entities.description: The federal government of the Republic of Arcasia. Presidential republic; unicameral Parliament of 150 seats.

entities.name: Bank of Arcasia
entities.description: Central bank and issuer of the Arcasian Koren (₳, ARK).

entities.name: ARC
entities.description: The Arcasian Republic Corporation. State holding company managing federal industrial assets.

entities.name: LEIKA
entities.description: Radios, televisions and household electronics from the Lachevan works. The pride of Arcasian living rooms.

entities.name: SATROM  [LCH]
entities.description: Lachaven branch of Saromese defence-electronics conglomerate. SATROM builds no weapons for the Republic... yet :)

entities.name: AMCO
entities.description: The Arcasian Mining & Carbon Oil company. Operates the Korota fields and the Razno refinery.

entities.name: ALKO
entities.description: Kordi-based mining and oil concern. Iron, copper and the K-7 field. The largest employer in the southern region.

entities.name: GRAZIHALL
entities.description: Construction and civil engineering house of the Grazi family. Built half of Port Kradon and most of its politics.

entities.name: United Arcasia
entities.description: Governing big-tent unionist party. Stability, industry and the federal idea.

entities.name: People’s Freedom and Justice
entities.description: Social-democratic opposition. Welfare, civil liberties and an open Republic.

entities.name: National Front
entities.description: Nationalist right. Order, the army and a hard line on Kordi and the strait.

entities.name: Arcasian Communist Party
entities.description: Marxist-Leninist party of the industrial belts. Watched closely by the ministry and by Qinal.

entities.name: Kordish Freedom Front
entities.description: Regionalist movement of the Kordish south. Autonomy now, and perhaps more later.

entities.name: Miron Valen
entities.title: President of the Republic
entities.description: Former leader of United Arcasia. A builder of compromises, so far.

entities.name: Ilya Verenne
entities.title: Leader of the Opposition (PFJ)
entities.description: Lawyer, orator, and the sharpest tongue in Parliament.

entities.name: Gregor Stahl
entities.title: Chairman, National Front
entities.description: Former colonel. Speaks softly about very loud things.

entities.name: Rosa Kandel
entities.title: First Secretary, ACP
entities.description: Veteran of the dock strikes of ’48.

entities.name: Aran Suri
entities.title: Chairman, Kordish Freedom Front
entities.description: Voice of Surat. Negotiates autonomy by day; the rest is rumour.

entities.name: Kira Moss
entities.title: Chief Executive, LEIKA
entities.description: Turned a radio repair shop into the Republic’s living-room monopoly.

entities.name: Viktor Hale
entities.title: Chief Executive, SATROM
entities.description: Engineer-director of the Razno works. Clearance level: considerable.

entities.name: Dana Keller
entities.title: Chief Executive, AMCO
entities.description: Oil heiress and operator. Knows the price of everything by the barrel.

entities.name: Baran Odek
entities.title: Chief Executive, ALKO
entities.description: Kordish industrialist. Employs half of Surat and funds the other half.

entities.name: Marta Grazi
entities.title: Chairwoman, GRAZIHALL
entities.description: Fourth generation of the Grazi construction dynasty.

entities.name: Pavel Orn
entities.title: Director-General, ARC
entities.description: Career administrator of the state corporation.

entities.name: Jana Halden
entities.title: Editor, The Arcasian Herald
entities.description: Runs the Republic’s paper of record from a smoke-filled office in Lachevan.

entities.name: Halvard Krenn
entities.title: Chief Justice
entities.description: Presides over the Supreme Court of the Republic.

entities.name: Gen. Petra Voss
entities.title: Chief of the General Staff
entities.description: Commands the armed forces of the Republic.

entities.name: Erik Falk
entities.title: Police Commissioner
entities.description: Federal police command, Lachevan.

entities.name: Toma Rill
entities.title: Citizen
entities.description: A citizen of Lachevan. Owns a radio, a television and 500 shares of AMCO.

entities.name: Valksland
entities.description: The great power across the Strait of Valgos. Watches Arcasia; Arcasia watches back.

entities.name: Del’ Casia
entities.description: Southern neighbour on the island. The land border is quiet this year.

entities.name: Solme
entities.description: Small south-western neighbour. Trades with everyone, angers no one.

entities.name: Madrosia
entities.description: Northern maritime republic. Old trading partner.

entities.name: Mazon
entities.description: Old agricultural Kingdom, a reliable source of food.

entities.name: Aldonesia
entities.description: South-western archipelago power.

entities.name: Federation of Sarom
entities.description: Faraway continental federation.

entities.name: People’s Republic of Qinal
entities.description: Revolutionary power.

entities.name: Kingdom of Markasia
entities.description: Old kingdom, older money. Buys Arcasian oil.

entities.name: Estal Federation
entities.description: Distant federation; occasional trade delegations.

entities.name: GRACE
entities.description: Guild of Royal Allies for Commercial Exchange. Trade bloc; Arcasia holds observer status.

entities.name: United Nations
entities.description: The United Nations. Arcasia is a founding member.

entities.name: Karaznia
entities.description: Northern neighbour beyond Mazon.

entities.name: Aragonia
entities.description: North-eastern power on the far shore of the border hills.

entities.name: Iceland
entities.description: A remote southern island territory, mostly fog.

entities.name: codey
entities.title: Citizen
entities.description: A citizen of the Republic.

entities.name: Dutch Wind
entities.title: Citizen
entities.description: A citizen of the Republic.

newspapers.name: Arcasia Today
newspapers.owner: This newspaper is owned by the State corporation ARC

newspapers.name: The National Herald
newspapers.owner: This newspaper is funded by the NFP

newspapers.name: Economists
newspapers.owner: This newspaper is owned by the Satrom group

venues.name: Satrom Grand Casino
venues.blurb: The Republic’s glittering house of chance, on the Lachevan strip.

venues.name: ARC National Lottery
venues.blurb: A flutter for the Republic. Drawn every third turn — pick 3 numbers, 1 to 40.

timeline.title: World initialised
timeline.detail: The Republic of Arcasia enters simulation. Five provinces, six great companies, one hundred and fifty seats, and a long decade ahead.

provinces.name: Grazi
provinces.description: North-western maritime province and the Republic’s foremost port region. Fjords, shipyards and the great deep-water Port of Kradon — the western gateway through which most Arcasian trade passes. Heartland of the Grazihall construction dynasty.

provinces.name: Lachevan
provinces.description: The federal heartland. Seat of government, the national airport and the LEIKA works. The most populous and prosperous province of the Republic.

provinces.name: Mezdov
provinces.description: Coastal western province where the highlands meet the sea. Fertile valleys and terraced farms run down to fishing harbours, while iron mines and quarries work the ridges above. An agricultural and mining region with a long tradition of hard labour and harder politics.

provinces.name: Korota
provinces.description: Landlocked industrial east, seated at Razno. Once the roaring heart of Arcasian manufacture and oil extraction — the old fields and the great refineries — the region has slid into stagnation, its plant ageing and its fields past their peak. No coastline: everything moves out by rail through Lachevan.

provinces.name: Kordi
provinces.description: Semi-autonomous, landlocked southern region. Dry inland plains over deep mineral and oil wealth, drilled by a growing forest of ALKO and AMCO wells. The Kordish question dominates its politics.

properties.name: Parliament of Arcasia
properties.description: Unicameral chamber of 150 seats, Lachevan.

properties.name: Presidential Palace
properties.description: Office and residence of the President of the Republic.

properties.name: Bank of Arcasia
properties.description: Central bank headquarters and the national gold vault.

properties.name: Lachevan International Airport

properties.name: University of Lachevan

properties.name: Herald House
properties.description: Offices and presses of The Arcasian Herald.

properties.name: Port of Cape Valgos
properties.description: Eastern harbour on the strait.

properties.name: Rill Residence
properties.description: A modest house with a very good television.

properties.name: Satrom Grand Casino
properties.description: The Republic’s glittering house of chance on the Lachevan strip, operated by the Saromese SATROM group.

properties.name: GRAZIHALL Headquarters
properties.description: The family seat above the Kradon docks.

properties.name: Port of Kradon
properties.description: Western deep-water port.

properties.name: Kradon Shipyards
properties.description: Hulls, cranes and welding sparks.

properties.name: GRAZIHALL Cement Works
properties.description: Feeds the Republic’s building sites.

properties.name: Northfield Collective Farm
properties.description: Grain and dairy on the northern coast.

properties.name: Mezdov Iron Mine M-2
properties.description: Iron ore from the western highlands.

properties.name: Western Quarry
properties.description: Granite and gravel for Grazihall sites.

properties.name: Fort Mezdov
properties.description: Mountain garrison of the 3rd Division.

properties.name: Mezdov Power Station
properties.description: Coal-fired plant supplying the western grid.

properties.name: SATROM Headquarters
properties.description: Razno offices of the defence concern.

properties.name: SATROM Electronics Works
properties.description: Precursor parts for precision instruments, built for export to the Federation of Sarom. Restricted site.

properties.name: AMCO Field Korota-1
properties.description: The Republic’s largest producing oil field.

properties.name: AMCO Razno Refinery
properties.description: Crude in, fuel out, smoke always.

properties.name: Valgos Naval Base
properties.description: Home of the Eastern Fleet, facing the strait.

properties.name: ALKO Headquarters
properties.description: Surat seat of the mining concern.

properties.name: ALKO Mine K-1
properties.description: Copper and iron under the dry plain.

properties.name: ALKO Field K-7
properties.description: The southern oil field. Politically combustible.

properties.name: AMCO Well K-9
properties.description: A cluster of oil wells on the eastern Kordi plain, newly drilled by AMCO.

properties.name: AMCO Well K-12
properties.description: Southern oil wells nodding over the dry plain. AMCO’s push into the Kordish fields.

properties.name: ALKO Well K-15
properties.description: A field of ALKO oil wells west of Surat, worked around the clock.

properties.name: Surat Grand Bazaar
properties.description: The commercial heart of the Kordish south.

properties.name: Fort Surat
properties.description: Southern garrison. Its presence is itself a policy.

properties.name: Kordi Aqueduct
properties.description: Waters the dry plain; built 1931, patched since.

properties.name: Mezdov Farm
properties.description: Largest farm in the republic

properties.name: Foreign Commerce Office
properties.description: The Arcasian Trade Office, manages everyday import/exports with international partners.
